themeName: Messages to Loved Ones / Future Generations

questions.text: What is one happy memory or simple story about your life that you’d love your children, grandchildren or loved ones to know?
What’s one piece of advice you’d give someone you care about deeply — something you wish you had heard earlier?
What are you really proud of in your life that you want your family to remember about you?
What song, book, place, tradition or object do you want to share with the people you love — and why?
What do you want your family to remember about the way you loved them?
Is there something you wish you had said to someone important to you while they were still here?
What values or ways of treating people do you most hope the next generation carries forward from you?
What forgiveness or understanding would you like to offer — or ask for — from someone you loved?
If this were one of the last messages your loved ones would have from you, what do you most want them to know?
What do you hope your great-grandchildren or future generations will say about who you were?
Is there a regret you want to release or explain to your family so they don’t carry it?
What do you want the next generations to do differently — or keep doing — because of the life you lived?
If you could leave one guiding truth or sentence for your family forever, what would it be?
What do you want people to feel when they remember you years from now?
Looking at your whole life — what is the one message you most want to pass on?
